headline: The Bear Serves Up Unexpected Origin Story in Surprise 'Gary' Prequel Drop
lede: Hulu's culinary drama The Bear just changed the game with an unannounced prequel episode that expands the show's gritty backstory. The surprise release promises to reveal crucial character origins.
body:
FX's critically acclaimed series takes an unconventional narrative turn with its surprise prequel episode, 'Gary', dropping suddenly on Hulu and offering fans an intimate glimpse into the backstories of key characters Carmy and Richard.

The unexpected installment features returning stars Ebon Moss-Bachrach and Jon Bernthal, who delve deep into the restaurant world's underground dynamics that preceded the original series' intense kitchen drama. Industry insiders suggest the standalone episode represents a bold storytelling experiment, potentially setting new precedents for streaming narrative structures.

Directed with the same kinetic energy that defined the show's initial seasons, 'Gary' appears designed to provide critical context about the relationships and professional tensions that ultimately shaped the Berzatto family restaurant ecosystem. Moss-Bachrach and Bernthal's collaborative performance promises to add nuanced layers to the already complex narrative landscape.

The prequel's sudden release underscores The Bear's reputation for narrative innovation, continuing the show's tradition of subverting traditional television storytelling conventions. Fans and critics alike are already dissecting the episode's implications for the series' broader mythology.
key_facts: Surprise prequel episode 'Gary' now streaming on Hulu
Features Ebon Moss-Bachrach and Jon Bernthal
Explores backstory of restaurant world dynamics
